rag_rules: Assume and guarantee statements must be placed in component type scopes, not implementation scopes.
References to component ports and subcomponents must be valid within the current scope or declared as helpers.
AGREE expressions must have valid types; unresolved references result in '<error>' types that must be fixed by resolving the reference or removing the clause.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be syntactically valid and refer only to visible identifiers or declared helpers.
Component-level contracts should not reference implementation-specific elements unless properly scoped.
Validator diagnostics about type mismatches or unresolved references indicate root causes that must be addressed by correcting declarations or removing invalid clauses.
When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all identifiers are resolvable within the current scope and that types match expected expressions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees must be declared in component types, not implementations.
Helper declarations (eq, const) must define valid types and refer to visible identifiers.
anti_patterns: Placing assume/guarantee statements in component implementations instead of types.
Using unresolved identifiers or invalid types in AGREE expressions without declaring them first.
Adding new architecture elements to fix AGREE diagnostics instead of resolving references or removing invalid clauses.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
Component type vs implementation structure in AADL
